title: pseudorchis albida
intro: Pseudorchis albida, also known as the pale adder's mouth, is a rare and endangered terrestrial orchid species found in the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Its health benefits are thought to include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is largely limited due to its rarity and potential toxicity.
